tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",

</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],

    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>
List all available schemas. Tell me what typenames are available.

content.text: The user wants to see all available schemas and their typenames.

extended.description: An address extension such as an apartment number, C/O or alternative name.

locality.description: The locality in which the street address is, and which is in the region.

region.description: The region in which the locality is, and which is in the country.

postOfficeBoxNumber.description: The post office box number for PO Box addresses.

country.description: The country in 2-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

content.text: Let me summarize the available schemas/typenames.

Here are all the available schemas and their typenames:

| # | Typename | Description |
|---|----------|-------------|
| 1 | **`com.example.type.expando`** | A flexible/generic object type that allows additional properties. |
| 2 | **`org.dxos.type.function`** | Represents a function with name, description, input/output schemas, services, and bindings. |
| 3 | **`org.dxos.type.organization`** | An organization with fields like name, description, status, image, website, and location. |
| 4 | **`org.dxos.type.person`** | A person/contact with fields like full name, emails, phone numbers, addresses, job title, organization, and more. |
| 5 | **`org.dxos.type.tag`** | A tag with a label and optional hue (color), used for tagging/labeling other objects. |
| 6 | **`org.dxos.type.trigger`** | A trigger configuration that can fire functions based on events like emails, queues, subscriptions, timers (cron), or webhooks. |

These are the six typenames currently available in the space. Let me know if you'd like more details about any specific schema or want to create/query objects of a particular type!

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

## DXOS — Research Summary

### Overview

<cite index="1-1,1-2">DXOS positions itself as "the new standard for building collaborative local-first software" and is an open source framework for building real-time, collaborative web applications.</cite> <cite index="9-1">DXOS stands for "Decentralized Operating System" — a platform for building fully decentralized applications.</cite> <cite index="1-3">DXOS applications run entirely on the client and communicate peer-to-peer, without the need for centralized servers.</cite>

### Company Details

- **Founded:** <cite index="17-1">DXOS was founded in 2021.</cite>
- **Headquarters:** <cite index="17-2">DXOS is headquartered in New York, NY.</cite>
- **Status:** <cite index="17-4">DXOS is a private company.</cite>
- **Industry:** <cite index="17-3">DXOS's primary industry is Software Development Applications.</cite>
- **Company Size:** 11–50 employees (per LinkedIn).
- **Investors:** <cite index="17-6">Backed VC, BlueYard Capital, Empty Set Group, and PL Capital (United States) have invested in DXOS.</cite>
- <cite index="4-6">DXOS was created by a small core team of developers from many countries.</cite>

### Mission & Vision

<cite index="6-3,6-4">DXOS is a developer of a decentralized platform designed to create the first functionally and commercially competitive, fully decentralized alternative to the corporate cloud. The company offers a network operating system, infrastructure platform, programming framework, and toolchain, enabling businesses to develop privacy-preserving applications for its users and a fairer marketplace for open-source developers.</cite>

<cite index="11-11,11-12">They are "setting a new standard for how modern software is built by reducing reliance on centralized servers and focusing on scalable peer-to-peer data networks." Their framework empowers anyone to create real-time collaborative tools within a privacy-focused, local-first workspace that is enhanced by AI capabilities.</cite>

### Core Technology Stack

The DXOS SDK consists of three main integrated components:

1. **ECHO (Eventually Consistent Hierarchical Object Store):**
   <cite index="1-6">ECHO is an open source database architecture that incorporates transparent data replication and conflict resolution for secure and scalable local storage.</cite> <cite index="25-16">ECHO is based on the Automerge CRDT, so both real time and offline collaboration is possible without manual conflict resolution.</cite>

2. **HALO:**
   <cite index="1-7">HALO seamlessly manages digital identities using public/private key pairs allowing nuanced control over access to applications, databases, and networked devices.</cite> <cite index="25-9">HALO is a decentralized identity and access control protocol that makes it easier to build collaborative applications.</cite>

3. **MESH:**
   <cite index="1-9">MESH extends existing internet protocols to enable secure and resilient peer-to-peer networks without the need for centralized server infrastructure.</cite>

### Technical Architecture

- **CRDT-based Sync:** <cite index="1-11,1-12">DXOS leverages Automerge, a powerful CRDT that enables multiple concurrent users to operate on the same data without loss or conflict.</cite>
- **Cloudless Design:** <cite index="1-14,1-15">The only reason you need a central server is to serve your application shell. Everything else happens in the client and works peer-to-peer.</cite>
- **WebRTC Networking:** <cite index="1-19">Data is automatically synchronized across networked devices using WebRTC for cloudless peer-to-peer networking.</cite>
- **Signaling:** <cite index="5-6">There are still signaling servers for peer discovery, but all data exchange happens peer-to-peer, and no servers have access to the data.</cite>
- **Reactivity:** <cite index="1-20">UI components are re-rendered automatically as the underlying data changes, with minimal impact on performance.</cite>
- **SDKs:** <cite index="4-12">The three components are wrapped together in a set of SDKs (currently, React and TypeScript) that enable developers to deliver sophisticated applications quickly.</cite>

### Flagship Product — Composer

<cite index="21-1,21-2">To show off the power of DXOS, they built Composer — a local-first workspace designed for small team collaboration.</cite>

<cite index="25-1">Composer is described as a "super-app for intelligence," where developers can collaborate in real-time, organize knowledge, extend with custom data structures, and run private machine learning and AI against their knowledge locally.</cite>

Key Composer features include:
- <cite index="21-13">Blending the power of popular file formats (docs, sheets, sketch & tables) while allowing users to build plugins, deploy custom functions, and leverage modern LLMs.</cite>
- <cite index="21-14">Real-time collaboration inside a modern markdown editor.</cite>
- <cite index="21-18">Custom plugin building to enhance functionality.</cite>
- <cite index="21-22">A local-first spreadsheet that supports custom functions.</cite>
- <cite index="21-26,21-27">Everything inside of Composer is a plugin, meaning users can leverage core functionality or build out whatever they need.</cite>
- <cite index="21-38">Users can import and export their entire database as plain, human-readable markdown, JSON, and media files.</cite>

<cite index="24-4,24-5">Composer is not yet ready for production; there are bugs, and breaking changes may occur.</cite>

### Agents

<cite index="7-4,7-5,7-6,7-7,7-8">DXOS is a decentralized and privacy-preserving platform which communicates peer-to-peer. When all the peers go offline, it's impossible for any new peers to replicate. Agents solve this problem — they are just like any other peer, but are assumed to have more uptime on the network, typically running on servers or desktop machines that are always plugged in.</cite>

<cite index="5-2">DXOS is also launching a fully-managed service to host agents</cite>, providing convenient off-site backup and persistent data availability.

### Open Source & Community

- <cite index="1-5">DXOS is fully open source.</cite>
- <cite index="3-2">DXOS has 64 repositories available on GitHub.</cite>
- <cite index="3-4">The primary codebase is the TypeScript implementation of the DXOS protocols, SDK, toolchain, and Composer.</cite>
- <cite index="1-23">The DXOS Community is made up of a diverse group of open source developers from all over the world.</cite>

### Key Differentiators

- **Local-first:** <cite index="12-2,12-3">DXOS applications work offline, share state instantly when online, and leave end-users in control of their data and privacy.</cite>
- **Privacy-preserving:** All data stays on user devices; servers never have access to user data.
- **No centralized infrastructure required:** Eliminates cost and constraints of traditional cloud hosting.
- **Peer-to-peer collaboration:** <cite index="4-41">Latency is incredibly low with no server round-trips; peers can still communicate over a local network even without internet.</cite>
- **AI-enhanced:** Supports running private AI locally against user knowledge.

### Current Status

<cite index="12-6,12-7">The DXOS SDK is under active development, with the current focus on building features and functionality that enables the development of Composer. The team is currently only able to provide limited support to developers using the SDK, and will prioritize suggestions and contributions that align with their work on Composer.</cite>
